hero.subline: Restore radiator performance, reduce boiler noise, and improve circulation with a full domestic system flush.

heroImage.alt: Engineer carrying out a domestic power flushing visit

trust.guaranteeText: Clear fixed quote before work starts

pricing.pricingDisclaimer: Power flushing cost depends on radiator count, sludge level, and required inhibitor/filter top-up.

faults.title: Cold spots
faults.description: Radiators heat unevenly with cold areas at the bottom.

faults.title: Boiler noise
faults.description: Rumbling or kettling sounds from trapped sludge and poor flow.

faults.title: Slow warm-up
faults.description: Heating takes too long to warm rooms even with thermostat on.

faults.title: Frequent bleeding
faults.description: Radiators repeatedly need bleeding and system balance drops.

faults.title: Blocked pipework
faults.description: Circulation problems from debris in older systems.

faults.title: High bills
faults.description: Inefficient circulation can increase gas use and running costs.

proofCards.reviewSnippet: All cold spots removed and system now heats quickly.
Engineer explained process clearly and left everything tidy.
Radiators warm evenly now and pressure is stable.

faqs.question: How long does a power flush take?
faqs.answer: Most domestic systems take between half a day and one full day depending on size.

faqs.question: How do I know if I need a power flush?
faqs.answer: Cold radiator spots, noisy boilers, and slow heat-up are common signs your system may benefit.
